steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage uses "Granted" to concede a point — the building's elaborate features couldn't literally go in a forest. The blank then introduces the counter-claim: natural influences ARE visible in Ferrer's design. The transition must signal contrast or concession: "even so" or "nevertheless."

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Furthermore,: adds information in the same direction as the previous statement. The passage is pivoting to a contrasting point, not piling on. ✗
  • Choice B — Still,: means "nevertheless" or "even so" — exactly the word that acknowledges the concession while introducing the contrasting observation about natural influences. ✓
  • Choice C — Similarly,: signals that something is like the preceding idea. The passage is contrasting, not drawing a parallel. ✗
  • Choice D — In other words,: signals a restatement or clarification of the same idea. The second sentence introduces a new, contrasting point, not a restatement. ✗
That is the correct answer.
